title: Matrix Builds in GitHub Actions
content:
### Matrix Strategy

A **matrix build** lets you test across multiple combinations of operating systems, language versions, and other variables — all with a single workflow definition.

### Basic Matrix

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        node: [18, 20]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test
```

This creates **6 jobs** (3 OS × 2 Node versions).

### `fail-fast`

Stops all matrix jobs when one fails:

```yaml
strategy:
  fail-fast: true
  matrix:
    node: [16, 18, 20]
    os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
```

### `include`

Add extra combinations or override variables:

```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    node: [18, 20]
    os: [ubuntu-latest]
    include:
      - node: 20
        os: windows-latest
        experimental: true
```

### `exclude`

Remove specific combinations:

```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    node: [16, 18, 20]
    os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    exclude:
      - node: 16
        os: windows-latest
```

### Use Cases for Matrix Builds

- **Cross-platform testing** — ensure the app works on Linux, macOS, and Windows
- **Multiple runtime versions** — test against different Node.js, Python, or Java versions
- **Environment variations** — test with different dependency sets or configurations
codeExample:
name: Matrix Build

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
        node: [18, 20, 22]
        include:
          - os: macos-latest
            node: 20
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

quiz.question: How many jobs does a matrix with 3 OS values and 2 Node versions create?
What does `fail-fast: true` do in a matrix build?
options: Stops all running matrix jobs when one fails
